_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the impl skill: does a real `/charness:impl` run honor its own central claims, above all that it routes to its references at the point of need? declaredReferences is the full reference set (coverage report). referenceEngagement classifies each reference on the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the locked methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). requiredCommandFragments are the engage-always references SKILL.md routes to in a representative run: if the run never opens one, it did not follow its own routing. thresholds are intentionally omitted until a first baseline capture sets a per-skill runtime budget. requiredCommandFragments rationale: These five form the core workflow spine: contract consumption (step 1), sequence discipline and design lenses for slice choice (step 3), verification ladder for proof strategy (step 4), and review gate for stop criteria (step 6); all are explicitly routed or directly instantiated in the workflow steps of a representative run.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Bootstrap section routes to this conditionally; fallback policy is 'continue with inferred defaults' so the representative run may omit it without losing honest path.
adapter-contract.md.trigger: When .agents/impl-adapter.yaml exists or needs creation to record repo-specific verification expectations.

contract-consumption.md.rationale: Step 1 workflow requires reading the current implementation contract or bootstrapping an inline one before implementation; this doc owns the interpretation rules for carrying forward fixed decisions and acceptance checks.

design-lenses.md.rationale: SKILL.md explicitly routes to this at line 10 and step 3 applies Beck and Ousterhout moves to choose interface simplicity and ownership boundaries for the slice.

external-api-contract.md.rationale: Step 4 routes here specifically to identify the mocked-test trap shapes that cause request/response mismatches; narrower than general verification strategy.
external-api-contract.md.trigger: When the slice adds or changes calls to a third-party API (Slack, GitHub, Notion, etc.).

review-gate.md.rationale: SKILL.md explicitly routes to this at line 10; step 6 'Run the stop gate' mandates fresh-eye review with minimum lenses (contract honesty, runtime behavior, boundary ownership, docs sync) before closeout.

sequence-discipline.md.rationale: SKILL.md explicitly routes to this at line 10 and step 3 uses it to choose the highest-leverage slice that avoids locking in adjacent detail prematurely.

spec-loop.md.rationale: Step 2 and closeout reference contract updates; this doc distinguishes normal slice learning (update contract inline) from concept drift (escalate back to spec).
spec-loop.md.trigger: When implementation reveals probe resolution or a fixed decision that turns out false and must be reclassified durably in the contract.

verification-ladder.md.rationale: SKILL.md explicitly routes to this at line 10; step 4 declares it 'owns the per-surface rules' as the governing lens for what proof to run in a representative implementation.
